title: Baklava Walnut Honey Layers

seo.title: Baklava Recipe: Walnut Honey Layers | Authentic Middle Eastern
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Baklava with sweet walnut and honey layers. This easy Middle Eastern dessert recipe brings the taste of tradition to your home. Perfect for special occasions!
longTailKeywords: how to make baklava walnut honey layers
easy baklava recipe with walnuts and honey
homemade baklava layers step by step
lowCompetitionKeywords: phyllo dough baklava with walnuts and honey
middle eastern dessert with phyllo and nuts
seo.h1: Authentic Baklava Walnut Honey Layers Recipe
h2Headings: What is Baklava Walnut Honey Layers?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Close-up of golden Baklava Walnut Honey Layers, glistening with syrup and topped with chopped pistachios.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, sweet flavors of homemade Baklava, featuring crispy phyllo, crunchy walnuts, and a fragrant honey syrup.

description: Baklava is a cherished Middle Eastern pastry, featuring delicate layers of crisp phyllo dough generously filled with finely chopped walnuts. Each bite offers a delightful crunch, soaked in a fragrant, sweet honey syrup that balances richness with aromatic spices. This traditional dessert is a true labor of love, perfect for celebrating special moments or simply indulging in a taste of heritage.

ingredients.notes: 1 cup for syrup, 1/4 cup for filling

instructions.title: Prepare the Walnut Filling
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the finely chopped walnuts, 1/4 cup granulated sugar, ground cinnamon, and ground cloves (if using). Mix well to ensure the ingredients are evenly distributed. Set aside.

instructions.title: Make the Honey Syrup
instructions.text: In a small saucepan, combine 1 cup granulated sugar and 1 cup water. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes. Remove from heat, stir in the honey and lemon juice. Let the syrup cool completely to room temperature. This is crucial for crispy baklava.

instructions.title: Prepare Phyllo and Pan
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Lightly grease a 9x13 inch baking pan with some of the melted butter. Unroll the phyllo dough carefully. Keep the phyllo sheets covered with a damp cloth to prevent them from drying out as you work.

instructions.title: Assemble the Baklava Layers
instructions.text: Place two sheets of phyllo dough in the bottom of the prepared pan, brushing generously with melted butter. Repeat this process with another two sheets of phyllo and butter. Continue adding layers, two sheets at a time, brushing with butter, until you have about 8-10 buttered phyllo layers for the base. Spread about one-third of the walnut mixture evenly over the phyllo.

instructions.title: Continue Layering with Walnuts
instructions.text: Cover the walnuts with two more buttered phyllo sheets. Spread another third of the walnut mixture. Repeat with two more buttered phyllo sheets, then the remaining walnut mixture. Finish by layering the remaining phyllo sheets on top, two at a time, brushing each pair generously with melted butter. Ensure the top layer is well buttered.

instructions.title: Cut and Bake
instructions.text: Using a sharp knife, cut the baklava into diamond or square shapes, cutting all the way through to the bottom of the pan. This step is easier before baking. Place the pan in the preheated oven and bake for 30-35 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and crispy.

instructions.title: Syrup the Hot Baklava
instructions.text: Immediately after removing the baklava from the oven, slowly and evenly pour the cooled honey syrup over the hot baklava. You will hear a satisfying sizzle. The hot baklava will absorb the cold syrup, ensuring crispy layers. Let the baklava sit at room temperature for at least 4-6 hours, or preferably overnight, to allow the syrup to fully absorb.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Once the baklava has fully absorbed the syrup and cooled, garnish with chopped pistachios if desired. Serve at room temperature and enjoy this delightful Middle Eastern treat.

tips: Authentic Middle Eastern cooking technique tip: Always thaw phyllo dough properly. It's best to move it from the freezer to the refrigerator overnight, then let it sit at room temperature for about an hour before using. Keep unused sheets covered with a slightly damp cloth to prevent them from drying out and cracking.

Key ingredient or substitution tip: For an even richer flavor and crispier texture, consider clarifying your unsalted butter before using it. This removes milk solids, leaving pure butterfat. For the syrup, a dash of rose water or orange blossom water can be added for a more traditional aromatic touch.

Storage or serving tip: Baklava tastes best after resting for several hours or overnight, allowing the layers to fully soak up the fragrant syrup. Store it at room temperature in an airtight container for up to two weeks. Do not refrigerate, as it can make the phyllo soggy.
article: Baklava Walnut Honey Layers is a beloved Middle Eastern Desserts dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
